message:
fix(lab): conformal calibration, explorer reports, RNG bias, scenario validation, spork shutdown

conformal.rs:
- Add debug_assert for alpha ∈ (0,1) in ConformalConfig::new and
  HealthThresholdConfig::new to catch invalid miscoverage rates at construction
- Fix calibration set double-counting: predict() was using
  n_calibration > min_calibration_samples to decide whether to add observations,
  but this skipped the first post-calibration sample and double-counted during
  the uncalibrated→calibrated transition. Now tracks was_already_calibrated to
  add exactly once
- Fix TwoSided threshold mode: nonconformity scores were computed from a stale
  median. Now recomputes scores from the current full-sample median so both
  calibration and test scores share the same reference point, preserving the
  exchangeability assumption required for the conformal guarantee

explorer.rs:
- Add Clone derive to RunResult so reports can include per-run details
- Explorer reports now include per-run results (was Vec::new() which discarded
  all run data, making post-hoc analysis impossible)
- Add tests for schedule/dpor/topology report completeness

injection.rs:
- Reset current_mode to Recording after run completes so callers can inspect
  the mode between sequential runs without stale state

instrumented_future.rs:
- Fix modulo bias in Fisher-Yates shuffle: `(rng_state as usize) % (i+1)` cast
  a u64 to usize before modulo, truncating on 32-bit targets. Now computes
  `(rng_state % ((i+1) as u64)) as usize` for correct distribution
- Fix off-by-one in random float generation: dividing by u32::MAX produces
  values in [0, 1] inclusive, but we need [0, 1). Dividing by (u32::MAX + 1.0)
  fixes this, preventing silent probability threshold violations

scenario.rs:
- Validate delay_min_ms ≤ delay_max_ms in chaos config (previously unchecked)
- Report ALL timestamp ordering violations in event timeline, not just the
  first (removed premature break)
- Validate cancellation count ≥ 1 when count-based strategy is used
- Validate probability ∈ [0.0, 1.0] for probabilistic cancellation
- Use entropy_seed (falling back to seed) for chaos RNG initialization so the
  chaos layer respects the separate entropy seed when configured

scenario_runner.rs / snapshot_restore.rs:
- Remove unnecessary .clone() calls on values that are moved immediately after

spork_harness.rs:
- Move run_until_quiescent() outside the app_handle.take() block so quiescence
  drain runs even when no handle exists (defensive correctness)
- In graceful_shutdown: always drain to quiescence even when stop() fails,
  honouring the structured-concurrency invariant. The stop error is propagated
  after draining so cleanup obligations are fulfilled before the error surfaces

virtual_time_wheel.rs:
- Replace wrapping next_timer_id increment with checked_add().expect() — timer
  ID exhaustion (2^64 timers) is a genuine logic error that should panic rather
  than silently wrap and produce duplicate IDs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
